mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls signals weakening labor market conditions, increasing recession risks and depressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS declines by > 50k in the prior month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) above 0.5% signals strong consumer demand, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline by > 1.5% in 60d windows where RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average indicates weakening manufacturing activity, creating headwinds for equity earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by > 1.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a broad-based economic slowdown, increasing risk-off sentiment and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 40% of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST decline by > 1% quarter-over-quarter.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 2.5% triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher real rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with declining payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a deteriorating labor market, prompting equity sell-offs and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp and PAYEMS declines > 50k.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 3-month moving average indicates strong manufacturing momentum, driving equity earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline by > 2% in 60d windows where INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5%.
